programmes.show_description: A dark and moody garage mix packed full of classic tracks and new school anthems to mark the end of Winter as we transition into brighter days!
6 Music takes you to the dark side with the finest goth, electronic and dark pop music, featuring Quiet Husband, SUMAC & Moor Mother, Violeta García, clipping. and more.
Kele takes a look back at the music that shaped his formative years!
Kele takes us on a trip to the pictures!
Clear your mind with this two hour mix of blissful ambient music.
Ease into your Saturday with AFRODEUTSCHE - the ultimate soft-launch for your weekend - two hours of fluid, deep-listening tracks designed to ground you before the day truly begins.
Mark and Stuart present a feast of great music and frontwoman of punk band Gossip, Beth Ditto, shares her 'First, Last and Everything' and chats about her new 6 Music show.
Deb Grant is back with her magical melting pot of great music to help you slide seamlessly into the weekend, including Beck, Os Mutantes, MAW, The Cure, Tribe, Jitwam and more!
Femi sits in for Jamz and is joined on the show by members of the band Nubiyan Twist, to speak about their new album 'Chasing Shadows'.
Nemone selects three hours of her favourite music. Plus, words from singer-songwriter Ego Ella May on her album Good Intentions and music from producer Romaal Kultan's new EP.

programmes.show_name: The Craig Charles Funk and Soul Show
programmes.show_description: Carwyn Ellis takes control of the Trunk of Funk. Flying the flag for Wales with serious style, he blends Welsh roots with Brazilian grooves.
Don's Saturday night selections cross time, space and genre. Expect some new numbers by the likes of Reggae Roast, Visions Of Light, Bella Monae, Gitkin and Gia Margaret.
SHERELLE welcomes Shy One to the studio to discuss her brand new album 'MALI', the importance of centring Black British artists, and releasing on the label Touching Bass.
Shy One enters the Sweatbox with an eclectic mix of dub, techno and grime.
(Some of) the best tracks to have soundtracked dance floors over the last 25 years!
Sink into the floor and relax with this hand selected two hour mix featuring Peter Hook, Childish Gambino and Clairo.
Two hours of chilled-out tracks from 6 Music Festival artists past and present. With a selection of slow songs from Jacob Alon, Bloc Party, Self Esteem, Thundercat and more.
Reclaim your Sunday morning with a curated journey from Afrodeutsche. From ambient textures to soulful grooves, it's the restorative musical flow you need to reset.
Mark and Stuart present a feast of great music and chat for weekend breakfast.
Ariadna Güell Sans from the Time Zone Initiative on time zone adjustments and Professor Fiona Douglas from the University of Leeds discusses a BBC archive of disappearing dialects.
Here Comes the Sun. A selection of summery sounds for a Sunday in spring.
Iggy serves up a sonic cocktail to soundtrack your Sunday afternoon. This week, he's got tunes by the likes of Zoh Amba, Swans, Wet Leg, Mary Chapin Carpenter and Motörhead.
DJ, author and host of 6 Music's Dreamtime, Zakia Sewell joins to select a track for the music library we're building for future generations End of Days.
Stuart's featured album is Weidorje's only album, their 1978 self-titled release. Plus, Autechre for acoustic guitar, Mermaid Chunky and Jan Dukes de Grey.
Zakia celebrates Spring Equinox with a load of folky selections. Expect some Pentangle, Donovan and Vashti Bunyan, plus we hear from the Black British Folk Collective.
